_meta.coordinate_source: Wikipedia, Survey of India, Google Maps verified
_meta.notes: All coordinates verified against Wikipedia infoboxes and cross-checked with Google Maps. Timings are standard and may vary on festival days.

temples.name: Somnath Temple
temples.significance: First and foremost of the 12 Jyotirlingas. One of the oldest temples in India, rebuilt 16 times.

temples.name: Mallikarjuna Temple (Srisailam)
temples.significance: Rare temple that is both a Jyotirlinga and a Shakti Peetha. Located in Nallamala Hills.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (220 km)

temples.name: Mahakaleshwar Temple
temples.significance: Only south-facing Jyotirlinga. Famous for Bhasma Aarti performed with sacred ash at dawn.
temples.nearest_airport: Devi Ahilyabai Holkar Airport, Indore (55 km)

temples.name: Omkareshwar Temple
temples.significance: Island shaped like Om (ॐ) on river Narmada. Houses two Jyotirlingas — Omkareshwar and Mamleshwar.
temples.nearest_airport: Devi Ahilyabai Holkar Airport, Indore (77 km)

temples.name: Kedarnath Temple
temples.best_time_to_visit: May to June; September to October
temples.significance: Highest of the 12 Jyotirlingas at 3,583 m. Part of Char Dham and Panch Kedar. Requires 20 km trek from Gaurikund.
temples.nearest_airport: Jolly Grant Airport, Dehradun (238 km by road)

temples.name: Bhimashankar Temple
temples.significance: Located in Sahyadri hills inside a wildlife sanctuary. Origin of river Bhima. Known for Giant Indian Squirrel (state animal).
temples.nearest_airport: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (115 km)

temples.name: Kashi Vishwanath Temple
temples.deity: Lord Shiva (Vishwanath — Lord of the Universe)
temples.significance: Most sacred Jyotirlinga. The new Vishwanath Dham corridor opened in 2021. Shiva is believed to grant moksha to those who die in Varanasi.
temples.nearest_airport: Lal Bahadur Shastri International Airport, Varanasi (25 km)

temples.name: Trimbakeshwar Temple
temples.significance: Origin of river Godavari. Linga has three faces representing Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva. Kumbh Mela held here (Nashik).

temples.name: Vaidyanath Temple (Baidyanath Dham)
temples.best_time_to_visit: July to August (Shravana Mela), October to March
temples.significance: Considered the most sacred abode of Shiva. During Shravana month, millions walk from Sultanganj (~110 km) carrying holy Ganga water.

temples.name: Nageshwar Temple
temples.deity: Lord Shiva (Nageshwar — Lord of Serpents) & Goddess Nagalai
temples.significance: First Jyotirlinga mentioned in Shiva Purana. Features a massive 25-metre statue of Lord Shiva.

temples.name: Ramanathaswamy Temple (Rameswaram)
temples.significance: Houses the longest temple corridor in the world (1,200 metres). One of the Char Dhams. Devotees take a ritual bath in 22 sacred teerthams (wells) inside.

temples.name: Grishneshwar Temple
temples.significance: Last and smallest of the 12 Jyotirlingas. Located near the UNESCO World Heritage Ellora Caves.

temples.name: Venkateswara Temple (Tirupati Balaji)
temples.best_time_to_visit: September to February (avoid peak festival rush)
temples.significance: World's richest and most visited religious site. Over 100,000 pilgrims visit daily. Part of 108 Divya Desams.
temples.nearest_airport: Tirupati Airport (15 km from base, Alipiri)
temples.nearest_railway: Tirupati Main Railway Station (22 km from Tirumala)

temples.name: Meenakshi Amman Temple
temples.significance: One of the most iconic temples in India. Features 14 gopurams (towers), 985 carved pillars, and the golden lotus tank. Around 15,000 visitors daily.

temples.name: Vaishno Devi Temple
temples.deity: Goddess Vaishno Devi (Maha Kali, Maha Lakshmi, Maha Saraswati)
temples.best_time_to_visit: March to October; avoid July–August (heavy rain)
temples.significance: Second most visited religious site in the world after Tirupati. Over 10 million pilgrims annually. A 14 km trek from Katra.

temples.name: Kamakhya Temple
temples.deity: Goddess Kamakhya (Devi Sati's womb — supreme Shakti)
temples.significance: Most powerful of all 51 Shakti Peethas. Marks the spot where Devi Sati's womb (yoni) fell. The temple has no idol — a natural rock fissure is worshipped.
temples.nearest_airport: Lokpriya Gopinath Bordoloi International Airport, Guwahati (23 km)

temples.name: Jagannath Temple
temples.significance: One of the original four Char Dhams (along with Dwarka, Rameswaram, Badrinath). World-famous Rath Yatra chariot festival. Non-Hindus not allowed inside.
temples.nearest_airport: Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (60 km)

temples.name: Badrinath Temple
temples.best_time_to_visit: May to June; September to October
temples.significance: Part of both Himalayan Char Dham and original Maha Char Dham. One of 108 Divya Desams. Temple opens for 6 months annually.

temples.name: Yamunotri Temple
temples.best_time_to_visit: May to June; September to October
temples.season_open: Akshaya Tritiya to Yama Dwitiya (Bhai Dooj)
temples.significance: Source of river Yamuna. Starting point of Himalayan Char Dham Yatra. Requires a 6 km trek from Janki Chatti.

temples.name: Gangotri Temple
temples.best_time_to_visit: May to June; September to October
temples.significance: Origin of river Ganga. Most accessible of the four Himalayan Dhams — road reaches the temple directly. Gaumukh glacier (actual Ganga source) is a 18 km trek further.

temples.name: Dwarkadhish Temple
temples.significance: One of the four original Char Dhams. The legendary capital of Lord Krishna. 72-pillar temple, 5-storey tall with a 78-metre tall shikhara.

temples.name: Padmanabhaswamy Temple
temples.significance: World's wealthiest temple by assets (treasure valuing over ₹1 lakh crore). Non-Hindus not permitted. One of 108 Divya Desams. Devotees must wear traditional attire (mundu/saree).

temples.name: Ranganathaswamy Temple (Srirangam)
temples.deity: Lord Ranganatha (Vishnu in Shesha Shayanam pose)
temples.significance: Largest functioning Hindu temple complex in the world (156 acres, 7 concentric precincts, 21 gopurams). Asia's largest gopuram (73 m). First of 108 Divya Desams.

temples.name: Brihadeeswara Temple (Thanjavur)
temples.significance: UNESCO World Heritage Site (with Gangaikonda Cholapuram & Airavatesvara). Built by Raja Raja Chola I in 1010 AD. The 66m vimana casts no shadow at noon.

temples.name: Amarnath Cave Temple
temples.season_open: Guru Purnima to Raksha Bandhan (approx 45 days)
temples.significance: One of the holiest Hindu shrines. A naturally occurring ice Shivalinga forms every year, waxing and waning with the lunar cycle. Located at 3,888m.
temples.trek_from: Pahalgam (48 km traditional route) or Baltal (14 km short route)
temples.nearest_airport: Sheikh ul Alam International Airport, Srinagar (100 km from Pahalgam)

temples.name: Shirdi Sai Baba Temple
temples.significance: One of the most visited religious sites in India with over 25,000 visitors daily. A spiritual saint revered by both Hindus and Muslims. Prasadalaya (free meals for 50,000 daily).

temples.name: Siddhivinayak Temple
temples.significance: Most visited Ganesha temple in India. Receives lakhs of devotees on Tuesdays. One of the wealthiest temples in Maharashtra. Trunk of Ganesha faces right (symbolising wishes granted with difficulty but permanently).
temples.nearest_airport: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (25 km)

temples.name: Lingaraja Temple
temples.significance: Largest temple in Bhubaneswar ('Temple City of India'). Built in 11th century Kalinga style. Non-Hindus not allowed inside but a viewing platform is available.
temples.nearest_airport: Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (3 km)

temples.name: Konark Sun Temple
temples.significance: UNESCO World Heritage Site. Conceived as a giant chariot of the Sun with 24 intricately carved wheels and 7 horses. Built by King Narasimhadeva I (1250 AD).
temples.nearest_airport: Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (65 km)

temples.name: Akshardham Temple (Delhi)
temples.architecture_style: Vastu Shastra-based, carved from Rajasthani pink sandstone & Italian marble
temples.significance: Guinness World Record — world's largest comprehensive Hindu temple complex. Opened 2005. Built with no steel — entirely traditional stone craftsmanship by 11,000 artisans.

temples.name: Ram Mandir (Ayodhya)
temples.architecture_style: Nagara (Chandrakanta Somapura design), carved from Rajasthani pink sandstone
temples.significance: Believed to be the birthplace of Lord Ram. Inaugurated January 22, 2024. One of the largest and most significant Hindu temples built in modern times.
temples.nearest_airport: Maharishi Valmiki International Airport, Ayodhya (10 km)

temples.name: Guruvayur Temple
temples.deity: Lord Guruvayurappan (Krishna as a child — Unnikrishnan)
temples.significance: Known as 'Dwaraka of the South'. One of the most important Vaishnavite temples in Kerala. The deity is Vishnu in four-armed form. Famous for temple elephant herd (Guruvayur Devaswom elephants).

temples.name: Sabarimala Temple
temples.darshan_timings: Open during Mandalam–Makaravilakku (Nov–Jan) and Vishu; limited other times
temples.significance: Over 50 million pilgrims during Mandalam season — one of the world's largest annual pilgrimages. Pilgrims observe 41-day Vratham (austerities). Accessible only after a 4 km forest trek.

temples.name: Khajuraho Temples (Kandariya Mahadeva)
temples.significance: UNESCO World Heritage Site. 85 original temples (25 survive), built by Chandela dynasty (950–1050 CE). Famous for erotic and devotional sculptural panels.

temples.name: Nataraj Temple (Chidambaram)
temples.deity: Lord Nataraj (Shiva as Cosmic Dancer) & Goddess Shivakami
temples.significance: One of the five Pancha Bhuta Stalas (Akasha/Space element). The 'Chidambara Rahasyam' (secret space) is the holiest spot — Shiva worshipped as infinite space.

temples.name: Ekambareswarar Temple (Kanchipuram)
temples.significance: One of the five Pancha Bhuta Stalas (Prithvi/Earth element). Features a 59-metre Raja Gopuram. Ancient mango tree (Sthala Vriksha) believed to be 3,500 years old.

temples.name: Madhyamaheshwar Temple
temples.best_time_to_visit: May to June; September to October
temples.significance: Second of the five Panch Kedar. Shiva is worshipped as a navel-shaped (nabhi/middle) lingam. Located at base of Chaukhamba Peak with stunning Himalayan views.
temples.nearest_airport: Jolly Grant Airport, Dehradun (280 km by road)

temples.name: Tungnath Temple
temples.significance: Third of the five Panch Kedar. Highest Shiva temple in the world. The nearby Chandrashila summit (3,679 m) offers a 360° Himalayan panorama.

temples.name: Rudranath Temple
temples.significance: Fourth of the five Panch Kedar. Most remote of all Panch Kedar. Shiva's face is worshipped here. Known for surrounding alpine meadows (bugyals) filled with wildflowers.

temples.name: Kalpeshwar Temple
temples.deity: Lord Shiva (Kalpeshwar — matted hair/Jata of Shiva)
temples.best_time_to_visit: Year round (only Panch Kedar open in winter)
temples.significance: Fifth and final of the Panch Kedar. Only one accessible year-round. Shiva's Jata (matted hair) is worshipped here inside a cave.

temples.name: Adi Badri Temple
temples.significance: Complex of 14 temples. The primary Adi Badri temple contains a 3-feet black stone idol of Lord Vishnu. Accessible year-round unlike Badrinath.

temples.name: Ashtavinayak — Moreshwar Temple (Morgaon)
temples.significance: First and last Ashtavinayak (circuit starts and ends here). The most important of the 8 Ganesha temples. Ganesha rides a peacock here.

temples.name: Kalighat Temple
temples.significance: One of the four major Shakti Peethas. Sati's right toes are believed to have fallen here. Over 2,500 years old. One of the 51 Shakti Peethas.
temples.nearest_airport: Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (20 km)

temples.name: Ambaji Temple
temples.significance: One of the four major Shakti Peethas. Sati's heart is believed to have fallen here. Bhavnath Mahadev (Shiva) is the Bhairava companion. Over 1.5 million visitors during Navratri.
temples.nearest_airport: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport, Ahmedabad (185 km)

temples.name: Mahalakshmi Temple (Kolhapur)
temples.significance: One of 51 Shakti Peethas (eye or breast of Sati). Sati's eyes are believed to have fallen here. Uniquely, sunlight falls directly on the idol's face twice a year.

temples.name: Murudeshwar Temple
temples.significance: Features the world's second-tallest Shiva statue (123 feet / 37.5 m). The 237-feet Raja Gopuram has a lift. Set dramatically against the Arabian Sea.

temples.name: Raghunath Temple (Jammu)
temples.significance: Largest temple complex in northern India. Built by Maharaja Gulab Singh in 1835. Contains images of deities from all over India. Houses one of the largest collections of Saligrams.

temples.name: Dakshineswar Kali Temple
temples.deity: Goddess Kali (Bhavatarini — She who liberates the universe)
temples.significance: Where Sri Ramakrishna Paramahamsa had his divine visions and spiritual realisations. Built by Rani Rashmoni in 1855. Directly linked to Sri Ramakrishna Math and Swami Vivekananda.
temples.nearest_airport: Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose Airport, Kolkata (15 km)

temples.name: Ujjaini Mahakali Temple (Nagpur)
temples.significance: One of the most revered Mahakali temples in Central India. Located at the geographic centre of India (Zero Mile). Visited by millions during Navratri.
temples.nearest_airport: Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar International Airport, Nagpur (8 km)

temples.name: Vitthala Temple (Pandharpur)
temples.best_time_to_visit: Year round; Ashadhi and Kartiki Ekadashi
temples.significance: The supreme pilgrimage of Maharashtra Varkari tradition. Millions walk barefoot in the Wari procession twice a year (Ashadha and Kartika). Vitthal stands with hands on hips (Katyavalambita pose).

temples.name: Jwalamukhi Temple
temples.best_time_to_visit: March to June; September to November
temples.significance: One of 51 Shakti Peethas — Sati's tongue fell here. No idol — nine eternal natural gas flames (of different colours) are worshipped as manifestations of Devi.

temples.name: Naina Devi Temple
temples.significance: One of 51 Shakti Peethas — Sati's eyes fell here. Reachable by ropeway from Anandpur Sahib side. Visited by over 1 million pilgrims annually.

temples.name: Chamundeshwari Temple (Mysuru)
temples.significance: One of 51 Shakti Peethas. Reachable by road and 1,000-step staircase. The Dasara festival here is a UNESCO Cultural Heritage event drawing millions.

temples.name: Vishalakshi Temple (Varanasi)
temples.significance: One of 51 Shakti Peethas. Located near the Dashashwamedh Ghat. Often combined with Kashi Vishwanath darshan.
temples.nearest_airport: Lal Bahadur Shastri International Airport (25 km)

temples.name: Birla Mandir (Jaipur)
temples.architecture_style: Modern Rajput-Mughal fusion in white marble
temples.significance: One of the most beautiful modern temples in India. Built by the Birla family in 1988. Made of pure white marble quarried from Makrana. Translucent at night.

temples.name: Pushkar Brahma Temple
temples.significance: One of the very few temples in the world dedicated to Lord Brahma. The holy Pushkar Lake (Sarovar) is one of the five sacred lakes in Hinduism.

temples.name: Eklingji Temple
temples.deity: Lord Shiva (Eklingji — one Linga God, family deity of Mewar royals)
temples.significance: Family deity of the Mewar royal family. Complex of 108 temples, all encircled by a fortified wall. The primary Shivalinga has four faces (Chatarmukhi) made of black marble.

temples.name: Vrindavan — ISKCON Prem Mandir
temples.significance: Inaugurated 2012. White marble temple with illuminated gardens featuring scenes from Krishna Lila. One of the most visited religious sites in Braj (Mathura-Vrindavan).

temples.name: Tripura Sundari Temple (Banswara)
temples.significance: Ancient temple of the tribal Wagad region. Major Shakti shrine of southern Rajasthan. Revered by both Hindus and Adivasis.

temples.name: Trident Temple — Ujjain (Kal Bhairav)
temples.deity: Kal Bhairav (Fierce form of Shiva — Kotwal of Ujjain)
temples.significance: Kal Bhairav is considered the guardian of Ujjain. Uniquely, wine (Sharaab) is offered as prasad to the deity — the only temple in India where alcohol is an official offering.
temples.nearest_airport: Devi Ahilyabai Holkar Airport, Indore (55 km)

temples.name: Kamakshi Amman Temple
temples.significance: One of the most important Shakti Peethas associated with Adi Shankaracharya, who established the Sri Chakra here. The main deity is seated in a majestic Padmasana posture.

temples.name: Dakshineswar Kali Temple
temples.significance: Founded in 1855 by Rani Rashmoni. Famously associated with the mystic Ramakrishna Paramahamsa who served as its chief priest.
temples.nearest_airport: Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose International Airport (12 km)

temples.name: Badrinath — Yogadhyan Badri
temples.best_time_to_visit: May to June; September to October
temples.significance: One of the Panch Badri temples. Legend says King Pandu (father of Pandavas) performed penance here to cleanse himself of a curse.

temples.name: Badrinath — Bhavishya Badri
temples.best_time_to_visit: May to June; September to October
temples.significance: Part of Panch Badri. Believed that in future, when the current path to Badrinath becomes inaccessible due to landslide (collapsing mountains of Nara and Narayana), Badrinath will be worshipped here.

temples.name: Badrinath — Vridha Badri
temples.significance: The first place where Lord Vishnu appeared as a child to Narada Muni. Located 7 km before Joshimath on the main highway.

temples.name: Badrinath — Dhyan Badri
temples.best_time_to_visit: May to June; September to October
temples.significance: One of the Panch Badri temples. Houses an image of Vishnu in meditative pose. Located in the beautiful Urgam Valley close to Kalpeshwar Temple.

temples.name: Ashtavinayak — Mayureshwar Temple
temples.significance: The starting and ending point of the holy Ashtavinayak pilgrimage. The deity is depicted riding a peacock, which slew the demon Sindhu.

temples.name: Ashtavinayak — Siddhivinayak Temple (Siddhatek)
temples.significance: Second temple of the Ashtavinayak circuit. The only deity of the eight whose trunk points to the right. Lord Vishnu is believed to have successfully defeated Madhu and Kaitabha after praying here.

temples.name: Ashtavinayak — Ballaleshwar Temple
temples.significance: The only Ashtavinayak temple named after a devotee (the young boy Ballal). Built in stone with columns arranged in a star pattern.
temples.nearest_airport: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (110 km)

temples.name: Ashtavinayak — Varadavinayak Temple
temples.significance: Famous for the oil lamp (Nandadeep) which has been burning continuously since 1892. Devotees can personally perform pujas here.
temples.nearest_airport: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (82 km)

temples.name: Ashtavinayak — Chintamani Temple
temples.significance: Ganesha is believed to have retrieved the wish-fulfilling jewel (Chintamani) for Sage Kapila from the greedy prince Guna here. Associated with Maratha statesman Madhavrao Peshwa.

temples.name: Ashtavinayak — Girijatmaj Temple (Lenyadri)
temples.architecture_style: Rock-cut Cave (Cave #7 of Lenyadri Buddhist Caves)
temples.significance: The only Ashtavinayak temple located inside a Buddhist rock-cut cave complex (Cave 7). Reached by climbing 307 steps. Goddess Parvati performed penance in these caves to conceive Ganesha.

temples.name: Ashtavinayak — Vighneshwar Temple (Ozar)
temples.significance: Ganesha defeated the obstacle demon Vighnasur here. The temple is surrounded by massive stone walls and has a golden dome.

temples.name: Ashtavinayak — Mahaganapati Temple (Ranjangaon)
temples.significance: Lord Shiva is believed to have worshipped Ganesha here before destroying the demon Tripurasura. The temple architecture is designed so that the sun rays fall directly on the idol during noon.

temples.name: Kamakhya Temple (Guwahati)
temples.significance: Center of Tantric Shaktism. Womb (Yoni) of Sati fell here. During the annual Ambubachi Mela, the river Brahmaputra adjacent is believed to run red during the Goddess's menstruation cycle.
temples.nearest_airport: Lokpriya Gopinath Bordoloi International Airport (20 km)

temples.name: Ananthapadmanabhaswamy Temple
temples.significance: The richest place of worship in the world. Deity is reclining on the multi-headed serpent Anantha in the 'Anantha Sayana' posture. Regulated strictly under royal trust of Travancore.

temples.name: Sarangapani Temple (Kumbakonam)
temples.significance: One of the three major chariot temples of Tamil Nadu. Deemed third in rank among the 108 Divya Desams. Features a 12-story high Raja Gopuram.

temples.name: Bhudevi Sametha Venkateswara Temple (Tirupati)
temples.significance: Considered the spiritual capital of Andhra. First of the 108 Divya Desams in worship scale. Most visited Hindu temple with over 30 million visitors annually.

temples.name: Arulmigu Parthasarathy Swamy Temple (Chennai)
temples.deity: Lord Parthasarathy (Krishna as Charioteer of Arjuna)
temples.significance: One of the oldest temples in Chennai, originally built by Pallavas in 8th century. Notable as the only temple displaying Krishna with a moustache and sans weapons (as he vowed not to take up arms during the Kurukshetra war).

temples.name: Thirukkurungudi Nambi Temple
temples.significance: One of the 108 Divya Desams. Believed to be the place where Lord Vishnu acted as a disciple to learn the Tamil scriptures from Nammalvar. Known for Kaiser Kalaikoodam wooden carvings.

temples.name: Nava Tirupati — Alwar Thirunagari Temple
temples.significance: Birthplace of Saint Nammalvar, the primary Alvar saint. It is one of the nine Nava Tirupati temples (associated with Planet Jupiter - Guru). Features a hollow tamarind tree inside, believed to have housed Nammalvar in deep meditation for 16 years.

temples.name: Adikesava Perumal Temple (Thiruvattar)
temples.architecture_style: Kerala Dravidian Wood and Stone Style
temples.significance: An ancient temple which predates Padmanabhaswamy Temple, built on a higher platform. The deity is 22 feet long and made of Katusarkara Yogam (a traditional herb-stone paste mixture).

temples.name: Sri Rangam Ranganathaswamy Temple
temples.significance: The largest functioning temple complex in the world by area (156 acres). The first and primary Divya Desam temple. Features 21 Gopurams including the tallest in Asia (Rajagopuram, 73m).

temples.name: Bhavani Sangameshwarar Temple
temples.significance: Located at the holy confluence (Triveni Sangam) of three rivers: Bhavani, Kaveri, and the invisible Amudha river. Devotees perform rituals here to appease ancestors.

temples.name: Brihadeeswarar Temple (Gangaikonda Cholapuram)
temples.significance: UNESCO World Heritage Site. Built by Chola Emperor Rajendra I to commemorate his victory over Ganga dynasty kingdoms. Houses a giant 4-meter tall single-stone Shiva Linga, the largest in South India.
special_events: Annabhishekam (rice decoration of the Linga in Oct/Nov)
pilgrimage_circuits: UNESCO Great Living Chola Temples Circuit

temples.name: Sri Ksheeraraamalingeswara Swamy Temple
temples.significance: One of the five Pancharama Kshetras of Andhra Pradesh. The temple features a unique white milk-coloured Shiva Linga, and its Gopuram is one of the tallest in Andhra, standing at 120 feet.

temples.name: Kailasa Temple (Ellora Caves)
temples.significance: The largest rock-cut monolithic structure in the world, carved out of a single basalt cliff face from top to bottom by the Rashtrakutas in the 8th century.

temples.name: Mukteshwar Temple (Bhubaneswar)
temples.significance: Regarded as the 'Gem of Odisha Architecture' (circa 950 AD) for its exquisite archway (Torana) and detailed ceiling carvings showing Ganesha and the Saptamatrikas.

temples.name: Sakshi Gopal Temple
temples.significance: Houses a life-size idol of Lord Krishna. Traditionally, pilgrimage to Puri Jagannath Temple is considered incomplete without visiting Sakshi Gopal to bear witness.
special_events: Anla Navami (Only day Radha's feet are displayed)

temples.name: Rajarani Temple (Bhubaneswar)
temples.significance: Built in the 11th century using local red-gold sandstone called 'Rajarani'. Known for its elegant carvings of Dikpalas (direction guardians) and erotic couples, it has no active worship or main idol today.

temples.name: Karni Mata Temple (Deshnoke)
temples.significance: Famous worldwide as the 'Rat Temple', housing over 25,000 sacred black rats (Kabbas). Seeing a rare white rat is considered extremely auspicious.
special_events: Karni Mata Fair (twice a year during Navratri)

temples.name: Salasar Balaji Temple
temples.significance: Highly revered temple of Lord Hanuman, uniquely depicted here with a full beard and mustache. It is believed the self-manifested idol was found in a farmer's field in 1754.

temples.name: Mehandipur Balaji Temple
temples.significance: Famous for spiritual healing and ritualistic exorcism of negative energies and evil spirits. Features three deities: Balaji, Pretraj Sarkar (King of Spirits), and Bhairav.

temples.name: Shrinathji Temple (Nathdwara)
temples.darshan_timings: Eight dynamic daily darshans (approx. 5:30 AM – 7:30 PM intermittently)
temples.significance: Principal shrine of the Pushtimarg sect, founded by Vallabhacharya. Depicts Lord Krishna lifting Govardhan Hill with his left hand. The idol was moved here from Vrindavan in 1672 to protect it from Aurangzeb.

temples.name: Udupi Sri Krishna Matha
temples.significance: Established in the 13th century by Saint Madhvacharya. Uniquely, the deity is worshipped not directly, but through a silver-plated 9-hole window called 'Kanakana Kindi' associated with the devotee Kanakadasa.

temples.name: Kukke Subramanya Temple
temples.deity: Lord Kartikeya (Subramanya as Lord of Serpents)
temples.significance: Famous destination for Sarpa Samskara and Ashlesha Bali (rituals to rid of snake curses). Lord Subramanya merged here with the serpent king Vasuki to protect him from Garuda.

temples.name: Dharmasthala Manjunatha Temple
temples.significance: Unique temple administered by a Jain family (Heggade), where the temple priests are Vaishnavite Madhva Brahmins, and the presiding deity is Shiva. Known for charity, providing free meals to over 10,000 visitors daily.

temples.name: Sringeri Sharada Peetham
temples.significance: The first of the four mathas established by Adi Shankaracharya in the 8th century. Features the Vidyashankara temple with 12 zodiac pillars designed so that the sun rays hit them in sequence according to the months.

temples.name: Tulja Bhavani Temple (Tuljapur)
temples.significance: Family deity (Kuldevi) of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj. One of the three and a half Shakti Peethas of Maharashtra. The self-manifested granite idol is dynamic and can be moved.

temples.name: Saptashrungi Temple (Nashik)
temples.architecture_style: Rock-cut hill shrine (with modern funicular trolley)
temples.significance: Located on a high hill with seven peaks (Saptashrunga). The massive 8-foot-tall idol of the Goddess is carved out of natural rock and has 18 arms holding various weapons.

temples.name: Chintpurni Devi Temple
temples.significance: One of the 51 Shakti Peethas. Sati's feet fell here. The presiding deity is worshipped in the form of a pindi (stone emblem) representing Chhinnamasta (the self-decapitated goddess).

temples.name: Bajreshwari Devi Temple (Kangra)
temples.significance: One of the 51 Shakti Peethas. Sati's left breast fell here. Historically looted for its immense gold treasures by Mahmud of Ghazni in 1009, and rebuilt completely after the 1905 Kangra earthquake.

temples.name: Tara Tarini Temple
temples.architecture_style: Kalinga Hilltop architecture (999 steps or ropeway)
temples.significance: One of the four major ancient Adi Shakti Peethas. Represents the breasts (Sthana Peetha) of Sati. Located on the Kumari hill banks of Rishikulya river.
special_events: Chaitra Mela (huge gathering on Tuesdays of Chaitra month)
temples.nearest_airport: Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (165 km)

temples.name: Biraja Temple (Jajpur)
temples.significance: Famous Shakti Peetha where Sati's navel (Nabhi) fell. Presiding deity is a two-armed image of Durga piercing the chest of Mahishasura. Navratri features a rare independent chariot festival (Rath Yatra) for the Goddess.
special_events: Shodasa Puja (16-day festival during Durga Puja)
temples.nearest_airport: Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (105 km)
temples.nearest_railway: Jajpur Keonjhar Road Railway Station (30 km)

temples.name: Danteshwari Temple (Dantewada)
temples.significance: One of the 51 Shakti Peethas. Sati's tooth (Danta) fell here. Presiding deity of the Kakatiya kings and Bastar tribes, carved in black stone.

temples.name: Kanakadurga Temple (Vijayawada)
temples.significance: Second largest temple in Andhra Pradesh, located on Indrakeeladri hill on the banks of Krishna river. Arjuna is believed to have performed penance to Lord Shiva here to obtain the Pasupatastra.
special_events: Dasara (huge festival with river bath processions)

temples.name: Tarapith Temple
temples.significance: Major Tantric center, famous for the adjoining cremation grounds and association with the saint Bamakhepa. Sati's eyeball/third eye (Tara) fell here.
temples.nearest_airport: Kazi Nazrul Islam Airport, Durgapur (95 km)

temples.name: Hanuman Garhi (Ayodhya)
temples.significance: A cave temple built in a fortress-like structure, custom dictates that before visiting the Rama Mandir, one must first pay respects to Hanuman here as he guards the city.

temples.name: Kanak Bhawan (Ayodhya)
temples.significance: Believed to be a palace gifted to Sita by Kaikeyi immediately after her marriage. Rebuilt in 1891 by Queen Vrishbhanu Kunwari of Orchha, the idols are adorned with gold crowns.

temples.name: Mundeshwari Devi Temple
temples.significance: Officially dated to 108 AD, it is recognized as the oldest functional temple in India, where worship has continued uninterrupted for nearly 2000 years. Famous for bloodless goat sacrifice where the goat is temporarily put to sleep by chanting mantra.
temples.nearest_airport: Lal Bahadur Shastri International Airport, Varanasi (100 km)

temples.name: Patan Devi Temple
temples.significance: The temple from which the city of Patna derives its name. One of the 51 Shakti Peethas. Sati's right thigh fell here. Divided into Badi (greater) and Chhoti (lesser) Patan Devi shrines.
temples.nearest_airport: Jay Prakash Narayan Airport, Patna (10 km)

temples.name: Deo Sun Temple
temples.significance: A rare monolithic stone Sun Temple that faces west instead of the traditional east. Dates back to the Umga dynasty (~15th century) with earlier foundations.
special_events: Chhath Puja (celebrated on a massive scale twice a year)
temples.nearest_railway: Anugrah Narayan Road Railway Station (20 km)

temples.name: Bhoramdeo Temple
temples.significance: Known as the 'Khajuraho of Chhattisgarh' due to its detailed erotic sculptured facades. Built by King Ramachandra of the Nagavanshi dynasty in the 11th century amidst the Maikal Hills.

temples.name: Six Abodes of Murugan — Subramaniyaswamy Temple (Tiruchendur)
temples.architecture_style: Dravidian Style (located on Bay of Bengal shoreline)
temples.significance: Second abode of Lord Murugan. Uniquely located directly on the seashore. Marks the place where Murugan defeated the demon Soorapadman.

temples.name: Six Abodes of Murugan — Dhandayuthapani Swamy Temple (Palani)
temples.architecture_style: Dravidian Hilltop style (693 steps, winch or ropeway)
temples.significance: Third abode of Lord Murugan. The main idol, created by Sage Bogar, is made of 'Navapashanam' (an alloy of 9 poisons that becomes healing medicine when bathed in milk).

temples.name: Six Abodes of Murugan — Swaminatha Swamy Temple (Swamimalai)
temples.architecture_style: Dravidian Hilltop style (60 steps representing 60 Hindu years)
temples.significance: Fourth abode of Lord Murugan. Marks the location where Murugan explained the meaning of the Pranava Mantra ('Aum') to his father Shiva.

temples.name: Six Abodes of Murugan — Thiruthani Murugan Temple (Thiruttani)
temples.architecture_style: Dravidian Hilltop style (365 steps representing days of the year)
temples.significance: Fifth abode of Lord Murugan. Represents the place where the Lord found peace and calm after defeating the demon Soorapadman, and where he married Valli.

temples.name: Six Abodes of Murugan — Solaimalai Murugan Temple (Pazhamudircholai)
temples.significance: Sixth and final abode of Lord Murugan. Celebrated as the site of the legendary 'fruit-seller' play between the poet-saint Avvaiyar and child Murugan under a rose-apple tree.

temples.name: Six Abodes of Murugan — Subramaniya Swamy Temple (Thiruparankundram)
temples.significance: First abode of Lord Murugan. A rock-cut temple carved into a massive granite hill. Marks the location where Murugan married Deivayanai, the daughter of Indra, in victory.

temples.name: Attukal Bhagavathy Temple
temples.significance: Famously called the 'Sabarimala of Women'. Holds the Guinness World Record for the largest annual gathering of women (over 3.7 million) cooking sweet Pongala offering on brick hearths around the city.
special_events: Attukal Pongala festival (10 days in Feb/March)

temples.name: Suryanar Kovil
temples.significance: Highly revered shrine dedicated to the Sun God (Surya) and other planetary deities. Features individual shrines for all nine planets.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Thingaloor Kailasanathar Temple
temples.significance: Famous Navagraha temple dedicated to Chandra (the Moon God). Devotees pray here to resolve lunar constraints and for intellectual success.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Vaitheeswaran Kovil
temples.significance: Lord Shiva is worshipped here as Vaitheeswaran, the Divine Healer. It is the Navagraha temple for Chevvai (Mars) and is famous for Nadi astrology.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Thiruvenkadu Swetharanyeswarar Temple
temples.significance: Presiding Navagraha temple for Budha (Mercury). The main deity is Shiva in the peaceful form of Swetharanyeswarar. Houses three holy water tanks.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Alangudi Apatsahayesvarar Temple
temples.significance: Highly popular Navagraha temple associated with Guru (Jupiter). The deity Shiva is revered as Apatsahayesvarar, who drank poison to save the world.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Kanjanur Agneeswarar Temple
temples.significance: The Navagraha temple representing Sukran (Venus). Lord Shiva is worshipped as Agneeswarar, and Sukran is believed to reside within the heart of Shiva.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Thirunallar Dharbaranyeswarar Temple
temples.significance: The most famous Navagraha temple dedicated to Lord Shani (Saturn). Devotees take a ritual bath in the Nala Theertham to ward off ill effects.

temples.name: Thirunageswaram Naganathar Temple
temples.significance: Navagraha temple for Rahu. Famous for the ritual bathing of Rahu with milk (Abhishekam), where the milk turns blue upon contact with the idol.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Keezhaperumpallam Naganathaswamy Temple
temples.significance: Navagraha temple dedicated to Ketu. Presiding deity is Naganathaswamy (Shiva), but the temple is primary for Ketu worship.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Arunachaleswarar Temple
temples.significance: Represents the Agni (Fire) element of the Pancha Bhuta Stala. Located at the base of the sacred Annamalai Hill, where Shiva appeared as a column of fire.
special_events: Karthigai Deepam (huge beacon lit on hill)
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Jambukeswarar Temple (Thiruvanaikaval)
temples.significance: Represents the Appu (Water) element. The sanctum features an underground water spring that remains wet, symbolizing Shiva as water.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Kalahasteeswara Temple (Srikalahasti)
temples.significance: Represents the Vayu (Air) element. The flame in the inner sanctum constantly flickers even when all doors are closed, representing Shiva as air.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Amaralingeswara Swamy Temple (Amaravati)
temples.significance: One of the five Pancharama Kshetras. Houses a massive 15-foot tall white marble Shiva Linga. Located on the banks of Krishna river.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Somalila Someswara Swamy Temple
temples.significance: Part of the Pancharama Kshetras. The Shiva Linga is unique because it changes color according to the phases of the moon (white on full moon, black on new moon).
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Draksharama Bhimeswara Swamy Temple
temples.significance: Pancharama temple known as the 'Dakshina Kashi' (Kashi of the South). The temple walls have detailed carvings dating back to the Eastern Chalukyas.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Kumararama Bhimeswara Swamy Temple
temples.significance: The final Pancharama temple. Features a monolithic two-story Shiva Linga that rises up to the first floor where darshan is performed.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Ahobilam — Jwala Narasimha Temple
temples.significance: Located in Upper Ahobilam on the hill of Achalachaya Meru. The spot where Lord Narasimha tore open the demon Hiranyakashipu.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Ahobilam — Ahobila Narasimha Temple
temples.significance: The main temple in Upper Ahobilam, where the self-manifested deity resides in a rock cave. It is the oldest of the nine temples.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Ahobilam — Malola Narasimha Temple
temples.significance: Houses Lord Narasimha in a peaceful form sitting alongside Goddess Lakshmi (Lola meaning beloved). Reached by trekking uphill.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Ahobilam — Kroda Narasimha Temple
temples.significance: Unique temple showing the deity as a combination of Varaha (the boar) and Narasimha.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Ahobilam — Karanja Narasimha Temple
temples.significance: Located under a Karanja tree. Lord Narasimha is depicted holding a bow and arrow, resembling Lord Rama.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Ahobilam — Yogananda Narasimha Temple
temples.significance: Lord Narasimha is depicted in a meditative posture, teaching yoga to Prahlada after the defeat of Hiranyakashipu.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Ahobilam — Chatravata Narasimha Temple
temples.significance: Located under a Peepal (Chatravata) tree. Lord Narasimha is depicted with a smiling countenance, enjoying music.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Ahobilam — Pavana Narasimha Temple
temples.significance: The most remote of the nine temples, located deep in the Nallamala forest. Requires a challenging trek or 4WD vehicle.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Ahobilam — Bhargava Narasimha Temple
temples.significance: Located near the Bhargava pond. Sage Parashurama (Bhargava) is believed to have performed penance here to see Lord Narasimha.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Devi Talab Mandir (Jalandhar)
temples.significance: Shakti Peetha where Sati's left breast fell. The temple features a sacred pond (Talab) which is believed to be over 200 years old.

temples.name: Bhadrakali Temple (Kurukshetra)
temples.significance: Shakti Peetha where Sati's right ankle fell. Devotees offer clay horses here upon fulfillment of wishes.

temples.name: Sharda Peeth Ruins
temples.significance: Ancient temple ruins that once housed the Sharda Peeth university. Considered the spiritual home of the Kashmiri Pandit community.

temples.name: Alopi Devi Mandir (Prayagraj)
temples.significance: Unique Shakti Peetha where no idol exists. Devotees worship a wooden palanquin (Doli) placed over a square platform.

temples.name: Vindhyavasini Devi Temple
temples.significance: Considered one of the most active Shakti shrines. Goddess Durga is believed to have resided here after slaying Mahishasura.

temples.name: Harsiddhi Mata Temple
temples.significance: Shakti Peetha where Sati's elbow fell. Features two massive 15-meter tall stone oil lamp towers (Deepasthambhas) lit daily during Navratri.

temples.name: Sharda Devi Temple (Maihar)
temples.significance: Located on the Trikuta hill. Associated with the legendary warrior brothers Alha and Udal who were first to worship Sharda here.

temples.name: Kamadgiri Temple (Chitrakoot)
temples.significance: A sacred forest hill believed to fulfill all wishes. Pilgrims perform a 5-km circumambulation (Parikrama) around the hill.

temples.name: Kaleshwari Art Temple Group
temples.significance: Ancient group of temple ruins featuring beautifully carved stepped ponds, panels of motherhood deities, and historical gateways.

temples.name: Chandrabhaga Shakti Peetha (Prabhas)
temples.significance: Shakti Peetha where Sati's stomach/abdomen fell. Located near the famous Somnath Jyotirlinga.

temples.name: Mangla Gauri Temple
temples.significance: Shakti Peetha representing the breasts of Sati. The temple is mentioned in ancient Puranas as a major center of Tantric power.

temples.name: Shakambhari Devi Temple
temples.significance: Devoted to Goddess Shakambhari (the Bearer of Vegetables/Plants) who fed hungry forest dwellers during a 100-year drought.

temples.name: Lalita Devi Temple (Naimisharanya)
temples.significance: Located at the holy forest of Naimisharanya. Shakti Peetha where Sati's heart fell.

temples.name: Kankalitala Temple
temples.significance: Shakti Peetha where Sati's waist/skeleton fell. The sacred pond adjoining houses the holy relics.

temples.name: Nalhateswari Temple
temples.significance: Shakti Peetha where Sati's throat/larynx (Nala) fell. Located on a small scenic hillock.

temples.name: Fullara Harihor Temple
temples.significance: Shakti Peetha where Sati's lower lip fell. A massive stone covered with vermilion is worshipped.

temples.name: Attahas Temple
temples.significance: Shakti Peetha where Sati's upper lip fell. The word Attahas means loud laughter.

temples.name: Bakreswar Temple (Mahamashini)
temples.significance: Shakti Peetha where Sati's portion between the eyebrows fell. Famous for its natural hot sulfur springs with therapeutic properties.

temples.name: Bahula Devi Temple
temples.significance: Shakti Peetha where Sati's left arm fell.

temples.name: Tripura Sundari Temple (Udaipur)
temples.significance: Shakti Peetha where Sati's right foot fell. Built by King Dhanya Manikya in 1501. The temple is shaped like a tortoise (Kurma Peetha).

temples.name: Keeriteswari Temple
temples.significance: Shakti Peetha where Sati's crown (Kirit) fell. One of the oldest temples in Murshidabad.

temples.name: Nartiang Durga Temple
temples.significance: Shakti Peetha where Sati's left thigh fell. Represents the ancient connection of Jaintia hills tribals with Shaktism.

temples.name: Vishweshwari Temple (Varanasi)
temples.significance: Shakti Peetha where Sati's earrings fell. Located near the main Kashi Vishwanath temple.

temples.name: Vadakkunnathan Temple (Thrissur)
temples.significance: Classic example of Kerala wood architecture, featuring giant gopurams (Kuttambalam). The Shiva Linga is covered under 10 feet of ghee offered over centuries, which never melts.

temples.name: Chengannur Mahadeva Temple
temples.significance: Famous for the monthly menstruation festival (Thrippoothu) of the Parvati goddess idol, a unique custom verified by temple authorities.

temples.name: Sreevallabha Temple (Thiruvalla)
temples.significance: One of the 108 Divya Desams. Famous for Kathakali performances held daily in the temple courtyard as a ritual offering to the Lord.

temples.name: Vaikom Mahadeva Temple
temples.significance: One of the oldest Shiva temples in Kerala. Associated with the Vaikom Satyagraha (1924) which successfully fought for temple entry rights for lower castes.

temples.name: Ettumanoor Mahadeva Temple
temples.significance: Famous for the display of eight golden elephants (Ezhara Ponnana) gifted by the King of Travancore. Wall paintings date back to the 16th century.

temples.name: Kadampuzha Bhagavathy Temple
temples.significance: Devoted to Goddess Parvati in her hunter (Kiratha) form. The deity has no idol; instead, offerings are made to a hollow pit (where the goddess disappeared).

temples.name: Chottanikkara Bhagavathy Temple
temples.significance: Worshipped as Saraswati in the morning, Lakshmi at noon, and Durga in the evening. Famous for curing mental ailments.

temples.name: Subrahmanyaswamy Temple (Payyanur)
temples.significance: Known as the 'Sabarimala of North Malabar'. Women are traditionally not allowed inside the inner wall (Chuttambalam) during certain hours.

temples.name: Srikanteshwara Temple (Nanjanagud)
temples.significance: Located on the Kapila river bank. Called 'Dakshina Kashi'. The deity was gifted a emerald necklace by Tipu Sultan after his royal elephant's blindness was cured.

temples.name: Melukote Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple
temples.significance: Important center of Sri Vaishnavism, closely associated with Ramanujacharya. Famous for the Vairamudi festival where the deity is adorned with a legendary diamond crown.

temples.name: Kollur Mookambika Temple
temples.significance: Established by Adi Shankaracharya. The deity is a golden line on a Swayambhu Linga (Shakti-Shiva integration). Highly popular for Vidhyarambham (education start) of children.

temples.name: Horanadu Annapoorneshwari Temple
temples.significance: Nestled in the Western Ghats. Every visitor, irrespective of background, is served a free three-course meal daily as prasadam.

temples.name: Kateel Durgaparameshwari Temple
temples.significance: Located on an island in the middle of the river Nandini. Famous for Yakshagana theatrical performances held as offering.

temples.name: Gokarna Mahabaleshwar Temple
temples.significance: Houses the sacred 'Atmalinga' of Lord Shiva, which Ravana was tricked into placing on the ground by Ganesha. Devotees perform ancestral rites on the beach.

temples.name: Banashankari Amma Temple (Badami)
temples.significance: Built by the Chalukyas in the 7th century. Located in the Tilakaranya forest. Famous for the Banashankari Jatre festival featuring chariot pulls and agricultural fairs.

temples.name: Badami Cave Temples
temples.deity: Lord Shiva, Lord Vishnu & Jain Tirthankaras
temples.significance: Group of four rock-cut caves carved out of red sandstone cliffs by the Chalukyas. Features the iconic 18-armed dancing Shiva (Nataraja) carving.

temples.name: Virupaksha Temple (Hampi)
temples.significance: UNESCO World Heritage Site. The oldest functional temple in Hampi, surviving the destruction of the Vijayanagara capital in 1565. Features a 50-meter high Gopuram.

temples.name: Hazara Rama Temple (Hampi)
temples.significance: A royal chapel in Hampi. Famous for the dense relief carvings on the outer walls depicting scenes from the Ramayana.

temples.name: Simhachalam Varaha Lakshmi Narasimha Temple
temples.significance: The deity is covered with sandalwood paste year-round, resembling a Shiva Linga. The paste is removed to reveal the true form for only 12 hours on Chandanotsavam.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Annavaram Satyadevi Temple
temples.significance: Famous across South India for performing the 'Satyanarayana Vratam' (ritual vow) for prosperity. Located on the Ratnagiri hill.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Dwaraka Tirumala Temple (Chinna Tirupati)
temples.significance: Known as 'Chinna Tirupati' (Lesser Tirupati). The self-manifested idol of Venkateswara is visible only down to the waist, with the lower body believed to be inside the earth.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Mahanandi Temple
temples.significance: Located in the Nallamala forest. Famous for its clear water tank (Kalyani) inside the temple complex, where water flows constantly from under the Shiva Linga.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Yaganti Uma Maheswara Temple
temples.significance: Famous for the growing stone Nandi (bull) idol. Archaeological Survey of India has verified that the Nandi increases in size over time. The temple caves were home to Saint Potuluri Veerabrahmam.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Lepakshi Veerabhadra Temple
temples.significance: Famous for its hanging pillar that does not touch the floor. Features the largest monolithic granite Nandi in India and beautiful 16th-century Vijayanagara frescoes.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Kanipakam Vinayaka Temple
temples.significance: The self-manifested Ganesha idol sits inside a well and is believed to continuously grow in size, having outgrown three silver kavachams (armors).
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Bhadrachalam Sita Ramachandra Swamy Temple
temples.significance: Located on the banks of Godavari river. Lord Rama is depicted in a rare four-armed posture (Chaturbhuja) holding a bow and a conch.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Yadadri Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy Temple
temples.significance: A major hilltop cave temple that was recently reconstructed completely using black granite stone (Krishnashila) without any cement.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Ramappa Temple (Mulugu)
temples.significance: UNESCO World Heritage Site. Built in 1213 AD by General Recharla Rudra. Famously named after the sculptor Ramappa. Constructed using floating bricks that float on water.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Thousand Pillar Temple (Warangal)
temples.deity: Lord Shiva, Lord Vishnu & Lord Surya (Rudreshwara)
temples.significance: Star-shaped temple built by King Rudra Deva. Features one thousand intricately carved pillars and a massive monolithic black basalt Nandi bull.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Gnana Saraswati Temple (Basar)
temples.significance: One of the very few active Saraswati temples in India. Devotees visit for the 'Akshara Abhyasam' ceremony to initiate children into education.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Vemulawada Raja Rajeshwara Swamy Temple
temples.significance: Known as 'Dakshina Kashi'. Uniquely, the temple complex houses both a Hindu shrine and a Muslim dargah of Baba Fasiuddin, showing communal harmony.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Chilkur Balaji Temple (Visa Balaji)
temples.significance: Famous as the 'Visa Balaji' temple, where IT professionals and students pray for visa approvals. Uniquely, it accepts no monetary donations and is not under government control.
temples.nearest_airport: Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (HYD) or local

temples.name: Dagadusheth Halwai Ganapati Temple
temples.significance: One of the most popular Ganesha temples in Maharashtra, founded by sweet maker Dagadusheth Halwai. The idol is adorned with nearly 8 kilograms of gold.
temples.nearest_airport: Mumbai Chhatrapati Shivaji (BOM) or Pune Airport

temples.name: Ganpatipule Swayambhu Temple
temples.significance: Located directly on the Ganpatipule beach. The Ganesha idol is a natural rock formation and faces west to guard the western coast.
temples.nearest_airport: Mumbai Chhatrapati Shivaji (BOM) or Pune Airport

temples.name: Harihareshwar Temple (Raigad)
temples.significance: Surrounded by four hills representing Shiva, Vishnu, Brahma, and Parvati. Known as the 'House of God' (Dev-Ghar) of Konkan.
temples.nearest_airport: Mumbai Chhatrapati Shivaji (BOM) or Pune Airport

temples.name: Khandoba Temple (Jejuri)
temples.significance: Dedicated to the warrior deity Khandoba. Famously known as the 'Yellow Temple' because devotees throw kilograms of turmeric powder (Bhandara) over the temple.
temples.nearest_airport: Mumbai Chhatrapati Shivaji (BOM) or Pune Airport

temples.name: Shani Shingnapur
temples.significance: The deity is a five-foot-tall black stone monolith. Uniquely, the entire village has no doors or locks on houses, believing Shani guards them.
temples.nearest_airport: Mumbai Chhatrapati Shivaji (BOM) or Pune Airport

temples.name: Kopeshwar Temple (Khidrapur)
temples.significance: Exquisite stone carvings on the banks of Krishna river. Features the 'Swarga Mandap' (heavenly hall) with an open circular roof to watch stars.
temples.nearest_airport: Mumbai Chhatrapati Shivaji (BOM) or Pune Airport

temples.name: Gondeshwar Temple (Sinnar)
temples.significance: A beautifully preserved 11th-century Panchayatana temple featuring a main shrine for Shiva surrounded by four smaller shrines.
temples.nearest_airport: Mumbai Chhatrapati Shivaji (BOM) or Pune Airport

temples.name: Ambarnath Shiva Temple
temples.significance: Built in 1060 AD. Carved entirely out of stone. The sanctum is underground and accessed by climbing down steps.
temples.nearest_airport: Mumbai Chhatrapati Shivaji (BOM) or Pune Airport

temples.name: Bhurshing Mahadev Temple
temples.significance: Scenic hilltop temple dedicated to Lord Shiva, located in the Solan valley. Known for panoramas of the Shivalik hills.

temples.name: Jakhoo Hanuman Temple (Shimla)
temples.significance: Located at the highest peak of Shimla (Jakhoo Hill). Features a giant 108-foot-tall orange statue of Lord Hanuman visible across the town.

temples.name: Hadimba Devi Temple (Manali)
temples.significance: A unique four-tiered wooden pagoda temple built in 1553, surrounded by dense cedar (Dhundri) forests. Worshipped as the patron deity of Manali.

temples.name: Vashisht Temple & Hot Springs
temples.significance: Dedicated to Sage Vashisht, the guru of Lord Rama. Famous for its natural hot sulfur springs where pilgrims bathe for health.

temples.name: Manikaran Shiva Temple
temples.significance: Located in the Parvati Valley. Famous for geothermal hot springs where food (rice and tea) is cooked directly in boiling springs.

temples.name: Baijnath Shiva Temple (Kangra)
temples.significance: Built in 1204 AD. Believed to be the spot where Ravana performed penance to Shiva. Uniquely, Dussehra (burning of Ravana) is not celebrated in Baijnath.

temples.name: Trilokinath Temple (Lahaul)
temples.significance: Unique temple revered by both Hindus (as Shiva/Trilokinath) and Tibetan Buddhists (as Avalokiteshvara), showing composite religious tradition.

temples.name: Shankaracharya Temple (Srinagar)
temples.significance: Perched on the Shankaracharya Hill. Visited by Adi Shankaracharya in the 8th century. The temple sits on an octagonal high platform.

temples.name: Martand Sun Temple Ruins
temples.significance: Ruins of a magnificent 8th-century Sun Temple built by King Lalitaditya Muktapida. Blends Gandharan, Greek, and Kashmiri architectural styles.

temples.name: Sudh Mahadev Temple
temples.significance: Houses a highly revered iron trident (Trishul) believed to belong to Shiva, which was used to slay the demon Sudheet.

temples.name: Khir Bhawani Temple (Ganderbal)
temples.architecture_style: Kashmiri marble pavilion in sacred spring
temples.significance: Presiding deity of the Kashmiri Pandit community. The water of the sacred spring under the temple is believed to change color to warn of future events.

temples.name: Tanot Mata Temple (Jaisalmer)
temples.significance: Located close to the Indo-Pak border. Maintained by the Border Security Force (BSF) after Pakistani bombs failed to explode here during the 1965 war.

temples.name: Dilwara Jain Temples
temples.significance: World-famous group of five Jain temples built between 11th and 13th centuries, celebrated for unmatched ornamental marble carvings.

temples.name: Achaleshwar Mahadev Temple
temples.significance: Houses a unique foot impression of Lord Shiva (worshipped instead of a Linga). Located close to the Achalgarh Fort.

temples.name: Ranakpur Jain Temple
temples.significance: A masterpiece of medieval architecture featuring 1,444 uniquely carved marble pillars, where no two pillars are alike.

temples.name: Bullet Baba Temple (Om Banna)
temples.significance: A unique folk shrine where a 350cc Royal Enfield motorcycle is worshipped. Travelers pray here for safe highway journeys.

temples.name: Govind Dev Ji Temple (Jaipur)
temples.significance: Located inside the City Palace. The deity was originally installed in Vrindavan and brought here by Raja Sawai Jai Singh II.

temples.name: Galtaji Temple (Monkey Temple)
temples.architecture_style: Rajput Haveli style with natural springs
temples.significance: Famous for its natural freshwater springs (Kunds) and hundreds of resident rhesus macaques (monkeys). Located in a mountain pass.

temples.name: Birla Mandir (Delhi)
temples.significance: Inaugurated by Mahatma Gandhi in 1939 with the condition that all castes be allowed entrance.

temples.name: Kalkaji Mandir (Delhi)
temples.significance: Ancient temple dedicated to Goddess Kali, believed to date back to the Mahabharata era.

temples.name: Chhattarpur Temple
temples.significance: The second largest temple complex in Delhi, spread over 60 acres with massive marble halls.

temples.name: ISKCON Temple Delhi
temples.significance: Features the Glory of India museum and one of the largest printed editions of the Bhagavad Gita.

temples.name: Sheetla Mata Mandir (Gurugram)
temples.significance: Revered shrine for Goddess Sheetla, visited by parents for the mundan (shaving head) of children.

temples.name: Agroha Dham
temples.significance: Major religious destination for the Agrawal community, featuring a massive temple complex.

temples.name: Mansa Devi Temple (Panchkula)
temples.significance: Located at the foothills of Shivalik. Established by the Maharaja of Mani Majra in 1815.

temples.name: Chaturbhuj Temple (Orchha)
temples.significance: A monumental fort-like temple built by Bundela Rajputs to house Lord Rama's idol (now in Ram Raja Temple).

temples.name: Ram Raja Temple (Orchha)
temples.significance: The only temple in India where Lord Rama is worshipped as a King (Raja) and receives a guard of honor daily.

temples.name: Pitambara Peetha (Datia)
temples.significance: One of the most famous Tantric centers dedicated to Goddess Bagalamukhi (the yellow-clothed goddess).

temples.name: Matangeshwar Temple (Khajuraho)
temples.significance: The only temple in the Khajuraho complex with active worship. Features an 8-foot-tall polished stone Shiva Linga.

temples.name: Kanch Mandir (Indore)
temples.significance: A unique Jain temple constructed entirely using glass beads, mirrors, and chandeliers imported from Europe.

temples.name: Bada Ganpati (Indore)
temples.significance: Famous for housing one of the largest Ganesha idols in the world, standing at 25 feet tall.

temples.name: Narmada Udgam Temple (Amarkantak)
temples.significance: Marks the source/origin of the holy Narmada River. Surrounding complex features beautiful white temples.

temples.name: Rajiv Lochan Temple (Rajim)
temples.significance: Ancient temple situated at the confluence of Mahanadi, Pairi, and Sondur rivers.

temples.name: Chandrahasini Devi Temple
temples.significance: Located on the banks of Mahanadi river. Famous for its cave-like path depicting mythological scenes.

temples.name: Bamleshwari Devi Temple (Dongargarh)
temples.significance: Perched on a hill 1,600 feet high. The temple is a major spiritual center of Chhattisgarh.

temples.name: Kapilash Shiva Temple
temples.significance: Located on Kapilash hill. Known as the 'Kailash of Odisha'. Built by King Narasinghadeva I in 1246 AD.

temples.name: Dhabaleswar Island Shiva Temple
temples.significance: Located on a beautiful island in the Mahanadi River. Reached by India's first pedestrian suspension bridge.
temples.nearest_airport: Domestic airport near Cuttack (Mahanadi River Island)

temples.name: Chausath Yogini Temple (Hirapur)
temples.significance: One of the very few surviving roofless circular Yogini temples in India. Features 64 miniature chlorite stone yogini statues.

temples.name: Kichakeswari Temple (Khiching)
temples.significance: The only temple in India made entirely of blue-black chlorite stone. Rebuilt from ruins in 1934 by Maharaja of Mayurbhanj.

temples.name: Gupteswar Cave Temple
temples.significance: A natural limestone cave temple situated in deep forests. Houses a self-manifested Shiva Linga which grows in size.

temples.name: Mahabodhi Temple (Bodh Gaya)
temples.significance: UNESCO World Heritage Site. Marks the location where Gautama Buddha attained enlightenment under the Bodhi Tree.

temples.name: Vishnupad Temple (Gaya)
temples.significance: Features a 40-cm footprint of Lord Vishnu stamped into solid basalt rock, surrounded by an octagonal silver basin.

temples.name: Jalmandir (Pawapuri)
temples.significance: Located in the middle of a lotus pond. Marks the site where Lord Mahavira attained salvation (Moksha) in 527 BC.

temples.name: Mahavir Mandir (Patna)
temples.significance: Highly popular Hanuman temple located outside Patna Junction. Features two unique Hanuman idols side-by-side.

temples.name: Singheshwar Stan (Madhepura)
temples.significance: Ancient Shiva temple mentioned in Varaha Purana. Popular destination during holy Shravan month.

temples.name: Hariharnath Temple (Sonepur)
temples.significance: Famous for the historic Sonepur Cattle Fair. Deity depicts Lord Shiva and Lord Vishnu fused as one.

temples.name: Chhinnamasta Temple (Rajarappa)
temples.significance: Located at the confluence of Damodar and Bhairavi rivers. Presides over the self-decapitated Goddess standing on Kamadeva.

temples.name: Jagannath Temple (Ranchi)
temples.significance: Built in 1691 by King Ani Nath Shahdeo. Modeled after the Jagannath Temple in Puri, it hosts its own annual Rath Yatra.

temples.name: Shikharji Temple (Parasnath)
temples.significance: The most sacred pilgrimage site for Jains, where 20 of the 24 Jain Tirthankaras attained salvation.

temples.name: Deori Mandir (Ranchi)
temples.significance: Houses a unique 16-armed stone idol of Durga. Highly popularised by cricketer MS Dhoni who visits regularly.

temples.name: Basukinath Temple (Dumka)
temples.significance: Devotees completing the pilgrimage to Baidyanath Dham visit Basukinath to offer Ganga water to complete the yatra.

temples.name: Belur Math (Howrah)
temples.architecture_style: Universal Architecture (incorporates Hindu, Christian, Islamic features)
temples.significance: Headquarters of the Ramakrishna Mission, founded by Swami Vivekananda. Built to symbolize the harmony of all religions.

temples.name: Adyapith Temple
temples.significance: Located near Dakshineswar. Famous for its three-tiered marble altar showing Ramakrishna, Radha-Krishna, and Adya Ma.

temples.name: Hangseswari Temple (Bansberia)
temples.significance: Unique 19th-century temple featuring 13 spires shaped like lotus buds, designed to represent the human body's Kundalini channels.

temples.name: Tarakeswar Mahadev Temple
temples.significance: Highly popular Shiva shrine. Millions of saffron-clad pilgrims carry Ganga water on foot from Sheoraphuli Ghat (38 km).

temples.name: Kapil Muni Temple (Ganga Sagar)
temples.significance: Located where the Ganges meets the Bay of Bengal. Millions take a holy dip here on Makar Sankranti day.
temples.nearest_airport: Domestic airport near Sagar Island (Ganga Sagar)

temples.name: Jalpesh Temple (Jalpaiguri)
temples.significance: Founded in 1524 by Koch King Biswa Singha. The underground sanctum houses a self-manifested Shiva Linga.

temples.name: Madan Mohan Temple (Cooch Behar)
temples.significance: Built by Koch Maharaja Nripendra Narayan in 1889. The focal point of religious life in Cooch Behar.
special_events: Rash Mela (largest festival in North Bengal)

temples.name: Sri Govindajee Temple (Imphal)
temples.significance: The royal temple of Manipur kings. Features gold-plated domes and a large paved assembly hall for traditional Manipuri Ras Lila dances.

temples.name: Malinithan Temple Ruins
temples.significance: Archaeological ruins of a 14th-century stone temple showing intricate carvings of Shiva, Surya, and Ganesha.

temples.name: Parasuram Kund
temples.significance: A sacred pool in the Lohit River where Sage Parasurama washed away his sins after performing his father's command.

temples.name: Unakoti Rock Carvings
temples.architecture_style: Bas-relief stone sculptures in forest hills
temples.significance: Famous forest hill features thousands of colossal rock-cut figures of Shiva dating to the 8th century. Unakoti means 'one less than a crore'.

temples.name: Hayagriva Madhava Temple (Hajo)
temples.significance: Revered by Hindus (as Vishnu's horse incarnation) and Buddhists (who believe Buddha attained Mahaparinirvana here).

temples.name: Umananda Temple (Guwahati)
temples.significance: Located on Peacock Island, the smallest inhabited river island in the world, in the middle of the Brahmaputra River.

temples.name: Maha Bhairav Temple (Tezpur)
temples.significance: Ancient temple believed to have been established by the demon king Bana. Houses a massive stone Shiva Linga.

temples.name: Sivadol (Sivasagar)
temples.architecture_style: Ahom style (Tallest Shiva temple in Assam)
temples.significance: Built in 1734 by Queen Ambika of the Ahom dynasty. Features a golden dome (Gorima) and stands 32 meters tall.

temples.name: Kirateshwar Mahadev Temple
temples.significance: Located on the banks of Rangeet River. Legend says Arjuna fought Shiva here in the guise of a hunter (Kirata).

temples.name: Siddhesvara Dham (Namchi)
temples.significance: Cultural park featuring a massive 108-foot-tall statue of Lord Shiva and detailed replicas of the pan-India Char Dham temples.

temples.name: Kapaleeshwarar Temple (Mylapore)
temples.significance: Famous temple in Chennai. Goddess Parvati is worshipped here in the form of a peahen (Mayil) which gave Mylapore its name.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Ashtalakshmi Temple (Chennai)
temples.significance: Located on Elliot's Beach. Features a unique multi-tier design allowing devotees to worship the 8 forms of Lakshmi sequentially.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Vadapalani Andavar Temple
temples.significance: Highly popular Murugan shrine in Chennai, closely associated with the South Indian film industry for marriages.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Varadharaja Perumal Temple (Kanchipuram)
temples.significance: One of the three major temples of Kanchipuram. Houses the famous 'Athi Varadar' (wooden deity made of fig wood) kept underwater in a temple tank, raised once every 40 years.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Kailasanathar Temple (Kanchipuram)
temples.significance: The oldest temple structure in Kanchipuram, built in the 8th century by the Pallavas. Known for its sandstone carvings of Shiva's dance postures.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Shore Temple (Mahabalipuram)
temples.significance: UNESCO World Heritage Site. One of the oldest structural temples in South India, built in 700 AD directly on the shores of the Bay of Bengal.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Srivilliputhur Andal Temple
temples.significance: Birthplace of Andal, the only female Alvar saint. The monumental 192-foot-tall temple tower (Gopuram) is the official emblem of the Government of Tamil Nadu.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Thanumalayan Temple (Suchindram)
temples.deity: Trimurti (Shiva, Vishnu, Brahma in one form)
temples.significance: Unique temple representing the Hindu Trinity (Than-u-mal-ayan: Shiva-Vishnu-Brahma). Features 4 musical pillars that emit musical notes when tapped.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Kanyakumari Bhagavathy Amman Temple
temples.significance: Located at the southernmost tip of mainland India (Triveni Sangam). Shakti Peetha representing the spine of Sati. The diamond nose-ring of the deity is globally famous.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Nagaraja Temple (Nagercoil)
temples.significance: The inner sanctum is a simple thatched mud roof. The temple sand turns black/yellow due to the continuous pouring of milk and turmeric over serpent idols.
special_events: Aavani festival (Sundays of Tamil month Aavani)
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Nellaiyappar Temple (Tirunelveli)
temples.significance: One of the largest temples in Tamil Nadu. The Tamra Sabha (Copper Hall) inside is one of the five cosmic dance halls of Shiva.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Papanasanathar Temple (Papanasam)
temples.significance: Located in the Western Ghats at the bank of Tambraparani river. Devotees take a dip to wash away sins (Papa-nasam).
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Kutralanathar Temple (Coutrallam)
temples.significance: Located near the famous Coutrallam waterfalls. Shaped like a conch shell (Sankha) representing the integration of Shaivism and Vaishnavism.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Sankaranarayanan Temple (Sankarankovil)
temples.significance: The deity depicts Lord Shiva (Sankara) on the right side and Lord Vishnu (Narayanan) on the left, demonstrating divine unity.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Uppiliappan Temple (Kumbakonam)
temples.significance: One of the 108 Divya Desams. The food offered to the deity is prepared strictly without salt (Uppillatha), hence the name Uppiliappan.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Airavatesvara Temple (Darasuram)
temples.significance: UNESCO World Heritage Site. Built by Raja Raja Chola II in the 12th century. The temple is shaped like a chariot drawn by horses and features musical steps.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Nageswaran Temple (Kumbakonam)
temples.significance: Built during the reign of Aditya Chola I (9th century). Designed so that the sun rays fall on the presiding deity only during three days of Chithirai.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Adi Kumbeswarar Temple (Kumbakonam)
temples.significance: The chief Shiva temple of Kumbakonam. The shape of the Shiva Linga is conical, representing the pot of nectar (Kumbha) that broke during pralaya.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Chakrapani Temple (Kumbakonam)
temples.significance: Dedicated to Sudarshana Chakra, the weapon of Lord Vishnu. The deity is depicted with eight arms holding weapons.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Ramaswamy Temple (Kumbakonam)
temples.significance: Referred to as the 'Ayodhya of Southern India'. The sanctum displays Rama and Sita on a single throne, surrounded by brothers.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Samayapuram Mariamman Temple
temples.significance: Highly popular Goddess shrine. Devotees offer salt, jaggery, and miniature metal parts of body organs to pray for healing.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Rockfort Ucchi Pillayar Temple
temples.significance: Perched on a 83-meter high rock. Marks the spot where Ganesha ran to escape King Vibhishana after establishing Srirangam.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Vayalur Murugan Temple
temples.significance: An ancient temple where the saint-poet Arunagirinathar was initiated by Lord Murugan to compose the Thiruppugazh hymns.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Thayumanaswamy Temple (Rockfort)
temples.significance: Located midway up the Rockfort hill. Shiva is believed to have taken the form of a mother (Thay-umanar) to attend the labor of a devotee.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Koodal Azhagar Temple
temples.significance: One of the 108 Divya Desams. The unique three-tiered sanctum displays the Lord in three postures: sitting, standing, and reclining.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Alagar Koyil Kallazhagar Temple
temples.significance: Located at the foot of Alagar hills. The golden idol of Kallazhagar is taken to Madurai during the Chithirai festival.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Thirumohoor Kalamegaperumal Temple
temples.significance: One of the 108 Divya Desams. Famous for the ornate shrine of Chakrathalvar (Sudarshana Chakra) depicted with 16 arms.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Mariamman Teppakulam Temple (Madurai)
temples.significance: A massive temple tank (largest in Tamil Nadu) featuring a central island temple dedicated to Lord Ganesha.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Kothandaramaswamy Temple (Rameswaram)
temples.significance: The only historical structure to survive the devastating 1964 cyclone. Believed to be the spot where Vibhishana surrendered to Lord Rama.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Devipattinam Navagraha Temple
temples.significance: Features nine stone pillars representing the Navagrahas, built directly inside the sea. Devotees perform rituals standing in sea water.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Adi Jagannatha Perumal Temple (Tirupullani)
temples.significance: One of the 108 Divya Desams. Lord Rama is depicted reclining on Dharbha grass (Sayana Rama) praying to the ocean god.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Dhanushkodi Kothandaramaswamy Temple Ruins
temples.significance: Ruins of the sacred temple located at the land's end, representing the starting point of Ram Setu.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Bannari Mariamman Temple
temples.significance: Highly popular Goddess shrine in the Western Ghats forest border. Famous for the fire walking ritual.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Magudeswarar Temple (Kodumudi)
temples.significance: Located on the banks of Kaveri river. Famous as a place where Shiva, Vishnu, and Brahma are worshipped together.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Chennimalai Murugan Temple
temples.significance: Perched on Chennimalai hill. Famous for the miraculous cart pull where a pair of bulls pull the cart without driver.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Masaniamman Temple (Anaimalai)
temples.significance: Features a unique 17-foot reclining clay statue of Goddess Masaniamman. Devotees grind red chilies and apply them to the stone to seek justice.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Eachanari Vinayagar Temple
temples.significance: Features a massive 6-foot tall Ganesha idol. Originally created for Madurai and installed here when the cart broke.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Marudamalai Murugan Temple
temples.significance: Located on a scenic hill part of the Western Ghats. Believed to be home to various siddhas and medicinal herbs.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Perur Pateeswarar Temple
temples.significance: Built by Karikala Chola. The Kanaka Sabha (Golden Hall) has rare stone pillars showing intricate carvings of Shiva's dance.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Karamadai Ranganathar Temple
temples.significance: The second oldest temple in Coimbatore. The deity is worshipped in the form of a self-manifested stone slab (Swayambhu).
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Avinashi Lingeshwarar Temple
temples.significance: Associated with Saint Sundarar, who performed a miracle to rescue a child from a crocodile's stomach.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Kodumanal Shiva Temple
temples.significance: Located close to the archeological excavation site of Kodumanal. Presides over ancient iron-melting furnace remains.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Tiruppur Tirupathi Temple
temples.significance: Built as a replica of Tirupati Venkateswara, popular destination in the Kongu region.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Namakkal Anjaneyar Temple
temples.significance: Houses a monumental 18-foot-tall monolithic Hanuman statue, standing directly under the open sky without a roof.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Namakkal Narasimhaswamy Temple
temples.significance: Carved into the side of the Namakkal Fort hill in the 8th century by the Pallavas. Features detailed carvings of Narasimha.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Ardhanareeswarar Temple (Tiruchengode)
temples.significance: One of the oldest hilltop shrines in South India. Dedicated to the composite half-male half-female form of Shiva and Parvati.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Salem Sugavaneswarar Temple
temples.significance: The deity was worshipped by Sage Patanjali and is believed to have been surrounded by parrot forests (Suka-vanam).
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Salem Kottai Mariamman Temple
temples.significance: The guardian deity of the Salem Fort. Famous for the annual festival featuring fire-walking.
temples.nearest_airport: Chennai International Airport (MAA) or Trichy Airport

temples.name: Belur Thanthonreeswarar Temple
